summary: A classical Minecraft structure featuring a central rectangular building with a gable roof, flanked by four tall corner towers with flat tops. The main body is constructed from light tan/cream colored blocks with regular window openings, while the roof is brick red. Four prominent pillars extend upward at each corner, each topped with a dark brown overhang. The entire structure sits on a raised dirt platform with white block foundations.
building_type: Classical mansion or palace with corner towers

shape.overall_form: Rectangular central block with four corner towers

rebuild_hints: Build central rectangular body first with sandstone blocks, approximately 10x8 blocks
Create regular 2x2 window openings in a grid pattern on all four walls across multiple floors
Construct four corner towers using dark wood pillars, extending 4-5 blocks above main roof
Add gable roof using brick blocks in a peaked configuration over the central structure
Place dark wood overhangs/caps on top of each corner tower
Build white quartz foundation blocks around the perimeter at ground level
Ensure bilateral symmetry is maintained throughout construction
Add interior floors between visible window rows
uncertainties: Exact block type for main walls appears to be sandstone or similar tan block, but could be concrete or terracotta
Interior layout and floor structure not visible from exterior views
Precise height of towers relative to main structure difficult to determine exactly
Whether towers are solid or hollow structures
Exact window frame material not clearly distinguishable
